prompt: A cinematic, dark gothic mystery movie poster. In the center, a lone man wearing a trench coat and fedora stands with his back to the viewer, looking up at a massive, imposing gothic clock tower. The clock face is illuminated, and the structure around it is shattering with dark fragments falling. The setting is a foggy, wet cobblestone square at night, illuminated by warm yellow streetlamps and glowing windows from surrounding European-style buildings. A full moon shines through cloudy skies. There are exactly 3 ravens: one perched on a stone monument on the left, and two flying in the sky. The monument on the left has engraved text "VERITAS IN UMBRA, TEMPUS NEMINI PARET". On the right, a wooden signpost has 3 directional arrows reading "Marktplatz", "Uhrenturm", and "Alte Gasse", with a small newspaper leaning against it reading "Die Uhr steht." and "Wer lügt?". The poster features elegant, slightly distressed serif Japanese typography. At the top, a horizontal tagline reads 真夜中、時は止まり、嘘が動き出す。. On the upper left, a vertical tagline reads その鐘の音が、真実を葬った。. In the lower center, the large main title reads 時計塔の殺人. Below the title is a smaller tagline "時を欺いたのは、誰だ。" followed by a dense block of realistic movie credits including director, cast, and crew names. At the very bottom, the release text 2025年 秋 全国ロードショー is displayed alongside a website URL and rating logos. The entire poster is framed by a thin, ornate gold border.
